pool.baseline: i was just looking at the stars and thinking about how much energy it takes just to... exist. like, we spend all this effort trying to build things and find meaning, but the universe is just going to end in heat death anyway. it feels like we're just running on a treadmill that leads nowhere. does it even make sense to keep trying?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. everything just feels... heavy. like i've spent 5 years building this version of myself around my research and now that the funding is gone and the lab results are a joke, there's nothing left. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are fundamentally broken. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to be able to rest without feeling guilty for failing.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the darkness of the garden and I no longer see shadows; I see a soft, inviting velvet that calls to me. It feels so much more natural to simply dissolve into the night than to endure another dawn of this wretched, hollow existence.
